frameworks.name: reward prediction error
frameworks.one_line: dopamine encodes scalar discrepancy between predicted and obtained reward

frameworks.name: mediated devaluation
frameworks.one_line: value of a sensory cue is updated via reactivated associative memory

frameworks.name: dopaminergic memory encoding
frameworks.one_line: dopamine encodes predictive representations of specific sensory reward features

core_question: Does midbrain dopamine encode sensory features of reward beyond a scalar reward prediction error?
key_assumptions: dopamine cell ensembles can be tagged during a learning phase and reactivated later
hedonic evaluation of sucrose indexes the cached value of reward memories
nucleus accumbens dopamine release indexes retrieval of reward representations
manipulations: chemogenetic reactivation of dopamine cells active during mediated devaluation
optogenetic and chemogenetic gain- and loss-of-function over dopamine cells
measurement of dopamine release in the nucleus accumbens during retrieval
key_findings: reactivating devaluation-active dopamine cells reduces hedonic evaluation of sucrose
dopamine cells are both sufficient and necessary for mediated devaluation
retrieval of devalued memories is accompanied by accumbens dopamine release
interpretations: dopamine carries predictive representations of specific sensory features of reinforcement
reward prediction error alone is insufficient to capture dopamine function
